title: Drift-aware prediction pipeline (Phase 4.5)

observation.what_i_noticed: 89.2% of crash model features (141/158) show critical drift, but drift detection was completely isolated from predictions. The crash model, signal engine, and all API endpoints operated as if features were in-distribution. This is the single biggest reliability gap: the system detects the problem but takes no action.
observation.root_cause: DriftDetector was built as an observability tool (Phase 4.4) but never wired into the prediction pipeline. The crash model has a reactive floor-pinning detector, but no proactive drift awareness.

what_i_did.summary: Built a drift-aware prediction pipeline that closes the loop: detect drift -> discount crash predictions -> reduce signal weight -> inform API consumers.
changes: Added config-driven drift confidence multipliers and signal weight multipliers to config.py
Created GET /api/drift/check endpoint (new drift router) with severity, confidence discount, and recommendations
Integrated drift detection into crash diagnostics endpoint (crash router) — status now reflects both calibrator health AND drift severity
Added drift metadata (severity + confidence_multiplier) to crash prediction response
Made signal engine drift-aware: crash_prob weight is reduced by a configurable multiplier based on drift severity (critical=0.2x, high=0.4x, moderate=0.7x)
Wired drift detection into market router, stock router, and lab data_generator — all share the feature matrix (no extra computation)
Drift severity surfaced in signal output when > 'none' (drift_severity, drift_crash_weight_mult fields)
Drift reason appears in signal reasons list when weight is discounted

test_classes: TestDriftAwareSignalWeighting (6 tests): weight reduction, monotonic effect, backward compat
TestDriftMetadataInSignal (4 tests): metadata presence, multiplier values, reasons
TestDriftConfidenceConfig (4 tests): config structure, severity ordering
TestBackwardCompatibility (3 tests): no-param, None, unknown severity

before.drift_detection: isolated — only saved to JSON in lab data generator
before.crash_predictions: no drift awareness, no confidence discount
before.signal_engine: crash_prob weight always 0.20 regardless of drift
before.api_consumers: no way to know predictions are unreliable
before.diagnostics: only checks calibrator floor-pinning, not feature drift

after.drift_detection: integrated into crash predictions, signals, and diagnostics
after.crash_predictions: include drift severity and confidence_multiplier in response
after.signal_engine: crash_prob weight reduced to 0.04 (0.20 * 0.2) under critical drift
after.api_consumers: GET /api/drift/check for drift status; drift metadata in all crash/signal responses
after.diagnostics: checks both calibrator health AND feature drift, returns combined status

results.impact_example: With 89.2% critical drift: crash_prob signal weight drops from 20% to 4% of composite signal. Other components (regime, momentum, drawdown, macro) that don't depend on the drifted model take proportionally more weight. This is correct: when you can't trust the ML model, lean on market observables.

design_decisions.config_driven: All multipliers in config.py — no hardcoded thresholds in service code. Easy to tune without code changes.
design_decisions.backward_compatible: drift_severity param defaults to None; all existing callers work unchanged. New field only appears in signal output when drift > 'none'.
design_decisions.feature_matrix_reuse: Drift detection reuses the feature matrix already built for crash predictions — no extra data fetching or feature computation.
design_decisions.graduated_response: Five severity levels (none/low/moderate/high/critical) with proportional weight discounts, not a binary on/off.
design_decisions.transparent: Drift reason appears in signal reasons list so users see WHY the signal changed, not just that it changed.

good: Addressed the single highest-impact reliability gap in the codebase
17 focused tests covering weighting, metadata, config, and backward compatibility
Zero existing test breakage across 175 verified tests
Clean separation: detection (DriftDetector) -> config (multipliers) -> integration (signal_engine) -> exposure (API)
limitations: Drift detection in market/stock routers adds ~2-5s latency for the DriftDetector.from_rolling_window() call. Should consider caching drift severity with a TTL.
The confidence_multiplier in crash prediction response is informational — the frontend doesn't consume it yet. Need frontend integration.
Did not address the root cause: the crash model needs retraining on recent data. This is a mitigation, not a fix.
Backtest service still calls get_market_signal without drift_severity — acceptable since backtests use historical data where drift wasn't an issue.

next_steps: Cache drift severity with 1hr TTL to avoid recomputing on every signal request
Frontend: show drift warning banner on crash prediction and signal pages
Retrain crash model with adaptive reference window to reduce drift
Add drift severity to lab data_generator output for experiment tracking
Consider auto-retraining trigger when drift exceeds 'high' for N consecutive checks
